review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Prestigious Projects: Hindustan Aeronautics Ltd. (HAL) is known for its involvement in prestigious aerospace and aviation projects, offering employees the chance to contribute to cutting-edge technology and innovation. Job Stability: HAL is a government-owned company with a strong presence in the aerospace and defense sector. This often translates to relatively stable employment, even during economic downturns. Skill Development: Working at HAL provides opportunities for skill enhancement in various fields including engineering, manufacturing, research, and more. The exposure to complex projects can foster valuable technical expertise. Networking Opportunities: Being part of HAL exposes employees to a diverse range of professionals and experts in the aerospace industry, leading to valuable networking opportunities. Career Growth: HAL offers employees a structured career path, enabling them to progress within the organization through promotions and skill-based advancements. Job Benefits: Employees often receive competitive compensation packages, benefits like medical coverage, pension plans, and allowances that contribute to a stable work-life balance.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: Bureaucracy: Being a government-owned entity, HAL may have bureaucratic processes that could slow down decision-making and project execution. Work Pressure: The aerospace and defense industry demands precision and adherence to strict standards, leading to potentially high work pressure and tight deadlines. Limited Autonomy: Employees might have limited autonomy due to the structured hierarchy and regulations within the organization. Technological Challenges: While HAL is involved in advanced projects, the pace of technological innovation can sometimes be slower compared to private sector counterparts. Location: HAL's facilities might be located in specific regions, potentially requiring employees to relocate or commute longer distances. Competition: The aerospace industry is highly competitive, and HAL might face competition from both domestic and international companies, affecting market share. Restrictions: Working with defense and aviation technologies might involve security clearance requirements, restricting personal activities and movements.

review-details_detailsLink__NxIv5: A Rewarding Experience as a Graduate Apprentice Trainee at Hindustan Aeronautics
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Hands-On Learning: As a Graduate Apprentice Trainee at Hindustan Aeronautics, one of the most rewarding aspects was the opportunity for hands-on learning. The organization provided a conducive environment for gaining practical experience, allowing me to apply the knowledge acquired during my studies to real-world projects. Industry Exposure: Working at Hindustan Aeronautics offered invaluable industry exposure. The company's prominence in the aerospace and defense sector provided a unique chance to witness and contribute to cutting-edge technologies and advancements. This exposure broadened my understanding of the industry and enhanced my professional growth. Mentorship and Guidance: The mentorship and guidance provided by experienced professionals at Hindustan Aeronautics were exceptional. From senior engineers to project managers, everyone was approachable and willing to share their expertise. Their support and feedback helped me develop essential skills and gain a deeper understanding of complex engineering concepts. Collaborative Work Environment: The work environment at Hindustan Aeronautics fostered collaboration and teamwork. I had the opportunity to work alongside talented individuals from diverse backgrounds, enabling me to exchange ideas, learn from others, and tackle challenges collectively. This collaborative spirit created a stimulating and enriching atmosphere. Growth Opportunities: Hindustan Aeronautics offered numerous growth opportunities during my tenure as a Graduate Apprentice Trainee. The organization encouraged professional development through training programs, workshops, and exposure to different departments. This commitment to personal growth laid a strong foundation for a promising career in the aerospace industry.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: Workload Intensity: At times, the workload as a Graduate Apprentice Trainee at Hindustan Aeronautics could be demanding. Balancing multiple projects and meeting deadlines required effective time management and prioritization skills. While challenging, this intensity also contributed to my professional growth and prepared me for future roles. Limited Autonomy: As an apprentice, there were instances where the level of autonomy was relatively limited. This was understandable considering the complex nature of aerospace projects and the need for adherence to established procedures. However, gradually gaining more responsibility and autonomy would have further enhanced the learning experience.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9:
1. No Work pressure. If you really don't have any opportunities at the moment you can consider going for this apprenticeship.
2. A place to be in if you are preparing for some other competitive examinations.
3. Nice and friendly employees.
4. Hands on exposure and an opportunity to spend time with deadly fighter aircrafts if you are in the right division.
5. Once in  a life time opportunity to visit and explore the facilities available at India's one of the top aerospace companies (OEM)
6.  You can build some really good networks if you wish to choose aerospace sector as your career domain.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
1.Typical government office environment. The pace at which employees work here is freaking slow. Sometimes you get pissed off seeing them work at such a slow pace.
2.Nobody will give a damn about your existence you can literally waste your whole day and return home . ( Not true for all divisions but applicable for majority of the divisions ) 
3. Highly discouraging employees as they believe that there are very less opportunities in the core aerospace sector.  BUT THAT IS THE REALLY HARD TO ACCEPT SAD TRUTH :( 
4. Do not waste your one year time for this apprenticeship if you really have some better option in hand.
5.In most of the divisions your are not asked to do any core engineering work. You would merely end up doing some data entry work like log book entry, file management(manual not digital ), part description documentation etc.  
6.Totally NON Competitive environment with average peer group.
7.The CORE technical knowledge that one would be learning is bare minimum. In certain cases you will not gain any technical knowledge at all!   
8. Hence most of the people start looking for some other opportunities and they try to switch,  sometimes even to different fields.

review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9: Premium aerospace industry of Asia. Work closely with defence forces and other top research agencies in the country and world.
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2: 6 day week, early start of shift, militant union and indifferent management. Recklessly insensitive work attitudes are pervasive across most levels which has affected the culture and resulted in trust levels drop to minimum. Extremely harsh HR  practices and policies. Appraisals and promotions are intransperant. The company even goes to the extent of harassing it's executives who resign, by refusing to issue relieving letters or causing inordinate delays. Offers extremely poor work life balance even for a psu. Executives invariably stay back for long hours to entertain their bosses who in turn stay back to impress theirs. Mobile phones are not permitted inside Divisions and whereever allowed permission is only for smartphones. Doesn't offer transport despite the early shift. Hospital is too full of hostile invonsiderate doctors and staff, that you would rather consult someone outside. Transfers and deployment are irrational and training needs are not scientifically arrived. Despite years of being in business HAL doesn't have SOPs nor internal coaches/mentors capable to addressing the day to day problems. Attrition is high am on entry and mid level execs yet management dismisses it as a triffle being only 2% overall.

1. Some of the R&D centers of this company provide good experience and exposure to new technologies to young officers in the area of aerospace and other disciplines. This kind of experience is most sought after by MNC's having projects of similar nature e.g aerospace and automobile.
2. Some of the Manufacturing  centers of this company provide good experience in state of the art manufacturing processes used for aircraft accessories. This kind of experience could be helpful in making career in core manufacturing industries especially  automobile related.
3. Company has a promising future in terms of business considering its huge order book of high valued defense projects and its monopoly in Indian market of rotary and fixed wing aircraft manufacturing .
review-details_isCollapsed__Bsg_9 2:
1. Salary and perk as per industry standards is dismally low compared to other Navratna/Mini Ratna PSU's. For young officers who are posted at Bangalore centers salary is too low to lead a comfortable life. No company accommodation and HRA of mere Rs 5000-6000(30% of Rs 16,400) for entry level engineer. And no overtime allowances for officers posted at shop floors.
2. Company has its own Hospital for employees and their dependents which is not well equipped 
    and entry level officers always have tough time in getting referred to private hospitals for critical health   conditions.
3. Companies top management has adopted a coercion policy for retaining its workforce since 6 years by way of 3-5 Lacs of bond effective for 6 years.
4. Each promotion takes 3-5 years and selection processes are often not transparent.  
5. Although a permanent officer is entitled to get NOC for applying to other Govt. jobs, company  higher officials often do not cooperate in this regard.
6. Company has too many hierarchies with centralized power which result in slower decision making at work place.
7. Company still follows HR practices and communication methods to facilitate production/Design which are archaic in nature.
8. Companies lacks brand name in public domain as well as among its primary customers owing to delayed product delivery.
9. All of the above facts have seriously demoralized its young workforce.
